# Project Context for GitHub Copilot

## Overview
We are working on a multi-vendor e-commerce platform called **Lebsey**. The goal is to transform this platform into a **reusable digital product template** that can be sold on multiple marketplaces. This template will allow anyone to create their own multi-vendor marketplace with features like vendor management, product catalogs, order processing, and a dynamic template system.

## Key Goals
- **Make the codebase clean, organized, and easy to customize.**
- **Ensure all components follow our established naming and folder conventions.**
- **Prepare the template so it can be packaged and sold on platforms like CodeCanyon or Gumroad.**
- **Focus on a professional structure that’s easy for other developers to understand and extend.**

## Technologies
- **Frontend**: Vike (SSR framework) + React 19, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui.  
- **Backend**: Fastify, tRPC, PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM, custom auth.

## Current Task
We’re currently working on cleaning up the component structure to ensure all components are organized into the correct subfolders and follow our naming conventions. We’re also removing any leftover test or placeholder components so the codebase is clean and ready for templating.”

You can paste that into a Markdown file and let Copilot “read” it, or just provide it as context so it understands the bigger picture. This way, Copilot will have that extra layer of context as it helps you code. Let me know if you need any adjustments!
